name: CURB-65
full_name: Confusion, Urea, Respiratory rate, Blood pressure, Age ≥65
description: A six point score based on confusion, urea, respiratory rate, blood pressure, and age to stratify adults hospitalised with community acquired pneumonia (CAP) according to increasing risk of 30-day mortality.
purpose: Stratify patients with community acquired pneumonia into different management groups based on severity at presentation to hospital.
when_to_use: Adults presenting to hospital with community acquired pneumonia
Assessment of CAP severity on presentation to hospital

items.label: blood_pressure
items.description: Low systolic (<90 mm Hg) or diastolic (≤60 mm Hg) blood pressure

interpretation.description: Group 1 (low severity): likely suitable for home treatment.
Group 2 (intermediate severity): consider hospital-supervised treatment (e.g., short-stay inpatient or hospital-supervised outpatient).
Group 3 (high severity): manage in hospital as severe pneumonia; assess for ICU admission especially if CURB-65 score is 4 or 5.

references.citation: Lim W, van der Eerden MM, Laing R, Boersma W, Karalus N, Town G, Lewis S, Macfarlane J. Defining community acquired pneumonia severity on presentation to hospital: an international derivation and validation study. Thorax. 2003;58(5):377-382.

notes: The score is based on information available at initial hospital assessment. The modified BTS rule (confusion, urea >7 mmol/l, respiratory rate ≥30/min, low blood pressure) was independently associated with mortality (OR 5.2, 95% CI 2.7 to 10). Age ≥65 (OR 3.5, 95% CI 1.6 to 8.0) was independently associated with mortality over and above the mBTS rule.
